Over the years, I’ve built and operated numerous production-ready Kubernetes environments across various industries — from highly regulated financial systems to modern entertainment platforms. I enjoy sharing knowledge about Kubernetes hardening, observability, and CI/CD best practices.

person.name: Hitesh Zope
person.bio: I'm a DevOps Engineer with a passion for automation and cloud-native technologies. I actively work with CNCF tools like Kubernetes, Prometheus, and ArgoCD to streamline infrastructure and deployment workflows. I also organize local DevOps meetups to share knowledge and connect with the community. My enthusiasm for open source and automation drives me to continuously explore innovative solutions in the DevOps space.

person.name: Jamal Ait Ahmed
person.bio: A few years ago, I decided to fully focus on cloud-native technologies, with one goal: to become a specialist in mission-critical platforms.  Today, I build, optimize and secure OpenShift and Kubernetes environments where reliability is essential. Along the way, I earned my Red Hat Certified Architect certification and completed the CNCF journey to become a Kubestronaut.  I don’t see certifications as the end goal. They are a confirmation of my expertise and a way to keep my knowledge up to date in a rapidly changing landscape.  I don’t know everything. Nobody does. But when I encounter something I don’t know, I dive in, figure it out and make sure I understand it. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and improve.  Automation first. Security first. Always learning. Always building. Always improving.

person.name: Jung-Hyun, Shim
person.bio: Hi, I'm a DevOps engineer. I am interested in technologies such as Private Cloud(Kubernetes), including Legacy Infra, and Public Cloud architecture design or Prometheus, Grafana, and Open Telemetry for services performance monitoring implementations.

person.name: Nathan Morrissey
person.bio: I come from a full-stack background, starting out with building progressive web apps in Angular before diving deeper into the platform and infrastructure side. These days, I’m leading a small team responsible for all Integration and Test activities on a training platform.  Outside of work, you’ll find me in my ever-expanding homelab — which seems to be outgrowing its 22U rack faster than I can keep up.

person.name: Viktor Ishchenko
person.bio: I’m a DevOps engineer who loves working with Kubernetes and everything around it. I have a strong passion for learning and exploring the CNCF ecosystem. Some of my favorite tools include ArgoCD, External Secrets, Helm, Istio and Kyverno. I enjoy automating workflows and improving cloud-native infrastructure.

person.name: Vishnu Kumar Sharma
person.bio: He is experienced and hands-on Software Developer and Architect, with a strong background in open-source development, DevOps, and Kubernetes. Passionate about building scalable, distributed, efficient software solutions. Known for blending architectural expertise with practical coding skills to drive impactful projects, streamline deployment processes, and optimize application performance. Skilled in designing cloud-native applications, managing CI/CD pipelines, and implementing container orchestration with Kubernetes. Eager to tackle complex challenges and innovate in dynamic tech environments.

person.name: Ayman Feki
person.bio: My name is Ayman Feki, and I’m a trainer specializing in DevOps and Kubernetes. I’m passionate about providing hands-on training that empowers individuals to gain practical, real-world skills. My goal is not just to teach but to create a collaborative and enriching learning experience built on open communication and a positive mindset. Whether you're just starting out or advancing your skills, I’m here to help you grow and succeed. Training with me is a shared journey, and I look forward to connecting and making a difference together in the DevOps and Kubernetes community!

person.name: Daniel Serrão
person.bio: I am an IT professional with over 10 years of experience, including 3 years as a Software Engineer and more than 7 years in Site Reliability, DevOps, and Platform Engineering roles. My expertise includes Kubernetes, Terraform, observability stacks (Prometheus, Thanos, Loki, Grafana, Alloy), and infrastructure tooling such as Helm and Kustomize, while not being limited to these technologies. I design and implement scalable, reliable, and secure platform solutions while mentoring engineers and driving best practices. With a proven track record in both architecture and hands-on delivery, I excel at bridging strategy with execution.

person.name: Dimitrios Synapalos
person.bio: Software Engineer with a focus on Public Cloud, DevOps and Cloud Native technologies.

person.name: Gustavo Ivo Meira De Carvalho
person.bio: I have been working as an SRE and Platform Engineer Expert in OpenSource tools for Observability such as Prometheus, Thanos, Cortex, Grafana, tempo, loki, Jaeger, Alertmanager.  I have more than 17 years in the IT area, experience in RISC and CISC architectures, VMware environments, I have worked in Disaster Recovery environments in public clouds and On Premises environments.  Currently working in environments with Containers in Kubernetes.

person.name: João Miguel Ribeiro Martins
person.bio: CNCF Enthusiast, Gitops & Platform Engineer

person.name: Richard Steinbiss
person.bio: Richard brings over seven years of consulting experience from Umlaut (part of Accenture) and Detecon (part of Deutsche Telekom), where he leveraged his data analytics and engineering skills to assist international clients in telecommunications and other industries. He holds a Master's degree with honors in Information and Communications Technology from RWTH Aachen University in Germany. Fully certified in Azure, AWS, and of course Kubernetes, Richard manages the data analytics and machine learning initiatives at OnRamp, a global fintech startup.

person.name: Łukasz Milczarski
person.bio: I am a technology enthusiast with a strong interest in Linux and Kubernetes. Currently working as a Senior Cloud Engineer, I enjoy continuous learning and have earned the Red Hat Certified Architect (RHCA) and OpenShift Architect certifications.

person.name: Enes Koc
person.bio: Enes Koç is a dedicated System Architect with a strong focus on Runtime and Cloud architecture. With an 18-year career in the industry, he specializes in Container Platforms, performance and optimization, monitoring and logging, and Infrastructure as Code (IaC). Enes holds the RHCA title and is now honored to join the Kubernetes community as a Kubestronaut.

person.name: Faseela K
person.bio: Faseela is a cloud-native developer, maintainer and steering committee member at Istio Service Mesh. She has served as the Program Chair for the Istio Meetups in 2023 including the IstioDays co-located with KubeCon Europe and NorthAmerica. She regularly contributes to the Istio blogs, helping all the latest news about Istio reach users and contributors worldwide.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: Having done the work of an ambassador for Istio for several years now, I look forward to continuing this work officially now, making use of the support CNCF provides. I would love to provide more support for the women in cloud native community with this new opportunity I am provided with.

person.name: Jooho Lee
person.bio: Jooho Lee is a DevOps Engineer at Devsisters Corp., where he focus on enhancing core infrastructure and devops platforms for game server. He is passionate about site reliability engineering (SRE), infrastructure as code (IaC), and continuous integration/continuous delivery (CI/CD) pipelines.

person.name: Mohamed Boulahcen
person.bio: I’m a Cloud & DevOps Engineer specializing in Azure, focused on building and automating scalable, secure cloud infrastructure. I’m passionate about cloud-native technologies, CI/CD, and modern DevOps practices.

person.name: Pierre Fort
person.bio: I am a tech enthusiast and IT professional with over 2 decades of experience, I enjoy collaborating with teams and clients on cloud-native solution architecture, DevOps, automation, and observability. I enjoy exploring new technologies and sharing my knowledge with colleagues through mentoring and coaching. The Kubernetes ecosystem is vast and always evolving, and I'm curious to see how AI-related workloads will change the game!

person.name: Aaron Zhilong Wang
person.bio: Zhilong Wang is a Principal Engineer specializing in cloud-native and distributed systems, with expertise in Serverless, Service Mesh, and cloud platforms. He has 10+ years of core architecture and practical engineering experience at Tencent, Alibaba, and JD.com, continuously improving the performance and reliability of cloud-native systems under data-intensive and compute-intensive workloads, including complex environments involving GPU/CPU heterogeneous resource coordination and mixed online–offline deployments. He also actively contributes to CNCF open-source projects such as Kubernetes, MOSN, and Layotto.

person.name: Abdelati Elasri
person.bio: Site Reliability Engineer with special interest in Kubernetes, GitOps, and the cloud-native ecosystem. Focused on CI/CD, and building scalable and secure infrastructure. Active member of the Moroccan tech community through Geeksblabla and Devsinstyle.

person.name: Ahmed Saad Hassan
person.bio: I'm Ahmed Saad, a passionate DevOps Engineer with strong expertise in Kubernetes (CKA, CKS, KCNA certified), CI/CD, and infrastructure automation using Terraform, Ansible, and Helm. I work extensively with cloud-native and container security tools like Trivy, Grype, and OPA, and manage scalable infrastructure on AWS and GCP. I'm currently expanding my skills into DevSecOps, with a focus on secure software delivery pipelines. I actively share knowledge through blog: ahmedsaadx.github.com and GitHub projects at github.com/ahmedsaadx. I'm also involved in the local DevOps and CNCF community through events and Meetups

person.name: Mike Bufano
person.bio: Mike is a Program Manager within Google’s Open Source Programs Office (OSPO), where he manages the strategic partnerships and foundation memberships that support Google’s open-source ecosystem. His work focuses on the intersection of ecosystem governance, corporate strategy, and community sustainability. Mike is also a dedicated proponent of driving diversity, equity, and inclusion initiatives across the technology industry. Outside of work, he loves bringing people together over a home-cooked meal, hunting for the best karaoke spots, and exploring the unexpected corners of New York City.

person.name: Oliver Paul K
person.bio: I am a Software Engineer passionate about building scalable, resilient applications with Kubernetes and open-source technologies. I enjoy solving complex challenges and improving developer experiences within DevOps and cloud-native ecosystems. With a focus on FinOps, I optimize cloud resources for both cost efficiency and performance. Currently, I am expanding into platform engineering and hybrid on-premise/cloud solutions while actively engaging with the tech community to learn and share knowledge.

person.name: Praveen Kumar
person.bio: I am a technology professional with an interest in cloud-native and modern infrastructure practices. I enjoy learning and working with containerized systems and distributed platforms, and I actively follow developments in the CNCF ecosystem. I am passionate about continuous improvement, knowledge sharing, and applying best practices to real-world engineering challenges. I value community-driven learning and staying up to date with emerging technologies.

person.name: Zhongjie Li
person.bio: I'm a DevOps&Platform engineer with a strong focus on Kubernetes and cloud-native technologies within the CNCF ecosystem. I specialize in automating infrastructure with Terraform, optimizing workloads on EKS, and implementing container security best practices using tools like Falco and Kyverno.

person.name: Hector Sebastian Mendoza Salazar
person.bio: I'm an IT Solutions Consultant with a degree in Network Administration, specializing in Kubernetes, Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform, DevOps, and API Management.

person.name: Kris Musard
person.bio: Kris is a seasoned technology leader and architect with nearly 30 years of experience, having recently focused on the tech-startup space. His passions include Cybersecurity, DevOps, and—of course—Kubernetes!

person.name: Nagaraju Varaparla
person.bio: Dedicated DevOps and Cloud Engineering professional with extensive experience building scalable infrastructure and deployment pipelines using Kubernetes and GCP. My expertise spans multiple programming languages including Python, Go and Ruby, allowing me to effectively bridge development and operations while implementing robust automation solutions. I'm passionate about container orchestration and platform engineering, consistently focused on optimizing infrastructure operations to enable teams to deliver software with greater velocity and reliability.

person.name: Francesco Grimaldi
person.bio: I'm a Team Leader at Desotech, specializing in cloud-native technologies for over 5 years. I've been drawn to the CNCF for a while now, and lately, I've been diving into organizing meetups in Southern Italy, both online and in person, to talk about various CNCF projects. I've also been visiting high schools around here, trying to spark interest in cloud-native tech among young students. Sharing my knowledge and getting others excited about these innovative technologies is what I'm all about!
